     The Numbers Business Report

The Water Diviner (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $4,200,117Details
International Box Office $31,200,000
Worldwide Box Office $35,400,117
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,941,167
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $648,022
Total $2,589,189
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Joshua Connor is an Australian farmer, who in 1919, goes in search of his three missing sons, last known to have fought against the Turks in the bloody Battle of Gallipoli. Arriving in Istanbul, he is thrust into a vastly different world, where he encounters others who have suffered their own losses in the conflict: Ayshe, a strikingly beautiful but guarded hotelier raising a child alone; her young, spirited son, Orhan, who finds a friend in Connor; and Major Hasan, a Turkish officer who fought against Connor's boys and who may be this father's only hope. With seemingly insurmountable obstacles in his path, Connor must travel across the battle-scarred Turkish landscape to find the truth...and his own peach.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$1,220,335 (29.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.44 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:11.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$22,500,000 (worldwide box office is 1.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:320 opening theaters/385 max. theaters, 4.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $5,958,891

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 24th, 2015 (Limited) by Warner Bros.
April 24th, 2015 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: December 26th, 2014 (Limited) (Australia)
December 26th, 2014 (Wide) (Turkey)
January 9th, 2015 (Wide) (Italy)
January 30th, 2015 (Wide) (South Korea)
March 13th, 2015 (Wide) (Poland)
Video Release: July 7th, 2015 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for war violence including some disturbing images.
(Rating bulletin 2366, 3/25/2015)
Running Time: 112 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Actors Making Their Directorial Debut, Directing Yourself
Film format: IMAX: DMR

Subgenre: Historical Drama

Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Hopscotch Features, Fear of God Films
Production Countries: Australia, United States, Turkey
Languages: English

Synopsis

Joshua Connor is an Australian farmer, who in 1919, goes in search of his three missing sons, last known to have fought against the Turks in the bloody Battle of Gallipoli. Arriving in Istanbul, he is thrust into a vastly different world, where he encounters others who have suffered their own losses in the conflict: Ayshe, a strikingly beautiful but guarded hotelier raising a child alone; her young, spirited son, Orhan, who finds a friend in Connor; and Major Hasan, a Turkish officer who fought against Connor's boys and who may be this father's only hope. With seemingly insurmountable obstacles in his path, Connor must travel across the battle-scarred Turkish landscape to find the truth...and his own peach.
